turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey, I was reading this article about how social media is ruining our attention spans. Like, we're all scrolling endlessly, can't focus on anything deep anymore. Totally agree with that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Yeah, I've seen those stats. Average attention span down to like 8 seconds now, shorter than a goldfish. It's wild.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Exactly! And it's all these algorithms feeding us bite-sized crap. No wonder nobody reads books anymore. We need to cut back, right? Go back to real conversations and stuff.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hold up, do you really think it's that bad? I mean, social media keeps me informed on news faster than TV. And I learn new things all the time from TikTok videos.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Sure, but that's surface-level. You can't really understand complex topics from 15-second clips. It's all superficial. Real knowledge comes from books or long articles.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Okay, but aren't you on Instagram like every day? Posting stories and stuff? How's that different from what you're criticizing?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Uh, well... yeah, but I use it mindfully. Just to stay connected with friends. Not like those doom-scrollers.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Mindfully? Weren't you telling me last week you spent three hours on Reels one night? Said it was 'research' for a meme or something.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: That was one time! And memes are cultural commentary, kinda deep if you think about it. Anyway, the point is the platforms are designed to addict us. I'm not addicted, though.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hmm. So earlier you said nobody reads books anymore because of social media. But aren't you in that book club that's basically just posting pics of books on Goodreads instead of discussing them?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Okay, fine, we do post pics, but we talk too! In the comments. It's modern discussion. Wait, no—actually, that's not the same. Real talk is face-to-face. Social media killed that.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Face-to-face? Dude, our whole friendship is half online now. You text me more than you call.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hesitation... yeah, texting is convenient, but it's not the same as... wait, isn't texting kinda like social media? Short bursts? Oh man, am I part of the problem? Nah, it's different because it's personal.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Personal short bursts that distract you all day? Sounds exactly like what you hate about apps. You're contradicting yourself here.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Ugh, maybe. I don't know. It's all so addictive. Pass me my phone—I need to check if there's a new study on this.

input.objective: Debate on social media's impact on attention spans, where A contradicts their own anti-social media stance
